name: Sinan ibn Anas
title: Ruthless Killer of the Pit
shortDescription: One of the executioners of Umar Sa'd's army who struck Imam Hussain (AS) with a spear in his final moments and played a major role in severing his head.
fullStory:
Sinan ibn Anas al-Nakha'i was one of the most violent and deranged criminals in the Kufan army. Even Umar ibn Sa'd recognized him for his madness and cruelty.

On the afternoon of Ashura, when Imam Hussain (AS) had fallen in the slaughter pit due to numerous wounds, the Kufan soldiers feared approaching the Imam. Sinan ibn Anas advanced with complete cruelty and thrust a long spear into the Imam's holy chest (or back).

Many historians believe that after Khawli trembled from severing the Imam's head, it was Sinan ibn Anas who severed the holy head of the Master of Martyrs. He proudly took the Imam's head to Umar Sa'd's tent and recited poetry to claim a reward, demonstrating his profound disbelief and ignorance.

Hearing his poem, which called Imam Hussain 'the best of people in parentage', Umar Sa'd got angry and said, 'If you know he is the best of people, why did you kill him?' Sinan was eventually captured after Mukhtar's uprising and punished in the worst possible way for his deeds.
quoteArabic: إِمْلَأْ رِكابِي فِضَّةً وَذَهَباً... أَنَا قَتَلْتُ السَّيِّدَ المُحَجَّبا...
quote: (Chanting while demanding a reward:) Fill my stirrups with silver and gold, for I have killed a great king (and the best of people).
